comment.body:
Interested in Alma’s weekend post, we didn’t reach the mean price she mentioned on the weekly time frame and there is still an enormous 6500 call wall on multiple duration time frames, we have two weeks of a decent balance range now.  ~6350-6480

Possible near term downside catalysts imo: NVDA earnings, PCE, August Jobs report.

comment.body:
There is no august jobs report since new Trump appointed labor head has said publicly jobs report will not be published. Market no longer cares about PCE since Fed has lost credibility. J Pow is famous for his transitory speech in 2021 when he called Inflation transitory and maintained rates. Then again in 2022 before reacting late and hiking rates. 

Fed used to say that Inflation target was 2%. We are nowhere near 2% and real inflation is 20% as Alma said in one of her posts. Yet  now same Fed is cutting rates thanks to the pressure from Trump and claims it is because of the labor market. 

It is all one big ponzi scheme since job numbers are distorted and will not be published. Fed claims labor is cracking and is cutting. Fed is silent about their desired 2% inflation target. 

So out of the 3 catalysts you mentioned for downside, 2 will not impact much. The remaining one is NVDA earnings and they will beat easily given that capex spending for Q2 did not slow down a bit.

comment.body: GARCH gives static over LSTM. LSTM has its own problems depending on the size of the dataset. Needs a lot of data, so a GRU might work better depending on what you’re trying to do with it

comment.body: Overcome the size of the dataset problem by building a time-series model to generate synthetic data with the same properties of financial data. Working on that myself rn and it’s tedious but a model that can essentially provide “infinite” accurate datasets is a great way to train/validate the NN’s and use real financial data as test sets
comment.raw_body: Overcome the size of the dataset problem by building a time-series model to generate synthetic data with the same properties of financial data.
